rulings.Case Title: AGONAFER SHIFERAW VS. JASON H. FRESKOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Dissolution Of Injunction And Release Of Bond
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday , March 13, 2017, line 1. PLAINTIFF AGONAFER SHIFERAW's Motion For Dissolution Of Injunction And Release Of Bond OFF CALENDAR. No Proof of Service re: Defendants Freskos and Sequoia Mortgage Capital LLC represented by Daniel McLaughlin. Additionally, moving party failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES,L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. JOSEPH KHALAF et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 13, 2017, line 2. PLAINTIFF 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES,L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to create a triable issue of material fact with competent admissible evidence. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Christina Mwangis Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 13, 2017, line 3. DEFENDANT CHRISTINA MWANGI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ Motion For Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs Complaint DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). See Notice of Motion and Separate Statement. Additionally moving party failed to cite any law or provide any evidence to support its argument that an owner of the property is not liable for failure to keep premises in a lawful state of habitability. Specifically no support is provided (evidentiary or legal) for the assertions that abatement of Notice of Violation in 77 days is "reasonable" as a matter or law (Note: Notice of Violation indicates that "all items must be completed within 30 days") or that the violations listed in the Notice of Violation are "trivial" not rising to the level of habitably breach. Finally, there is a triable issue of fact regarding Defendant's liability under SFRO 37.10B. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TYLER MIRANDA ET AL VS. LORETTA BOZUNG ET AL (Pltfs/X-Defts: Miranda and Weisbarth Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Tyler Mirandas Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Loretta Bozung; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,600.00 Against Defendant Loretta Bozung
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 13, 2017, line 5. PLAINTIFF TYLER MIRANDA's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Loretta Bozung; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,600.00 Against Defendant Loretta Bozung transferred to Department 301 to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on March 14, 2017. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL A. DEKELAITA ET AL VS. GAYLE MCKENZIE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 13, 2017, line 8. Defendant Gayle McKenzie's Motion To Strike Complaint GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fraud and request for punitive damages with requisite particularity. The Court notes that Defendant failed to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to allege fraud with particularity is subject to a motion to strike. All cases cited address fraud cause of action on a demurrer. Plaintiff did not object to this procedural issue. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BOWEN ET AL VS. ELIZABETH BREUILLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 13, 2017, line 6. DEFENDANT ELIZABETH BREUILLY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to attach a complete copy of the notice served (including portions required by SFRO) or to allege exemption from SFRO. = (501/AJR)
